nodes.label: MedAgentBench
nodes.summary: A benchmarking framework designed to evaluate the agent capabilities of large language models within medical record contexts.

nodes.label: Large Language Models
nodes.summary: Advanced AI systems that use deep learning techniques to understand, generate, and interpret human language.

nodes.label: FHIR
nodes.summary: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources, a standard for electronic exchange of healthcare information.

nodes.label: Agent Orchestrator
nodes.summary: A system component that manages interactions between the agent, the LLM provider, and the electronic medical record environment.

nodes.label: Electronic Medical Record
nodes.summary: Digital versions of patients' paper charts that provide real-time, patient-centered records accessible instantly and securely to authorized users.

nodes.label: Claude 3.5 Sonnet v2
nodes.summary: A state-of-the-art large language model evaluated as the best performer on the MedAgentBench dataset.

nodes.label: AgentBench
nodes.summary: A generic benchmark framework for evaluating agent capabilities in general applications.

nodes.label: Health Interoperability
nodes.summary: The ability of different information technology systems and software applications to communicate, exchange data, and use the information that has been exchanged.

nodes.label: BFCL
nodes.summary: A framework mentioned for agent orchestration inspiration in MedAgentBench.

nodes.label: James Zou
nodes.summary: A researcher mentioned who has contributed to studies on agentic AI in healthcare.

nodes.label: Andrew Y. Ng
nodes.summary: A notable computer scientist and author of the paper.

nodes.label: Interactive Healthcare Environment
nodes.summary: An environment that simulates real-world healthcare settings for AI agents to perform tasks interactively.

nodes.label: Administrative Task Automation
nodes.summary: The use of AI systems to reduce the administrative burden on healthcare staff by automating routine tasks.

nodes.label: Agent Capabilities
nodes.summary: The abilities of AI systems to perform tasks proactively by interpreting instructions, interacting with systems, and refining responses.

nodes.label: Stanford Research Repository
nodes.summary: A clinical data warehouse used to extract deidentified patient data for MedAgentBench.
